title: Agarose Gel Electrophoresis for DNA Analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5eo7h","text":"This protocol describes the preparation and execution of agarose gel electrophoresis for DNA analysis. The method is used to separate DNA fragments based on size, using an electric field in an agarose gel matrix. The gel is stained with ethidium bromide (EtBr) for visualization under UV light. This protocol follows strict safety guidelines for handling EtBr, a known mutagen. Proper personal protective equipment (PPE), designated waste disposal, and workspace decontamination are required.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e2lgr","text":"EtBr is a mutagen—handle with gloves and avoid cross-contamination between contaminated and uncontaminated areas.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a8gvp","text":"All EtBr waste (gels, gloves, tips, and tubes) must be disposed of in a designated biohazard waste container.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"137oj","text":"Ensure proper voltage selection based on the agarose gel concentration and DNA fragment size.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vem9","text":"Always confirm gel orientation—the wells must be on the negative (black) electrode side.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bpj24","text":"Use a fresh pipette tip for each sample to prevent cross-contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1624","text":"Do not exceed recommended runtime to avoid running DNA off the gel.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"duevg","text":"Prepare a clean and decontaminated workspace to minimize contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bgave","text":"Ensure all materials are pre-labeled before starting the gel preparation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3o0dg","text":"Wear PPE (lab coat, gloves, safety goggles) and handle EtBr-stained materials with caution.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"djnfo","text":"Confirm all power supply settings before starting electrophoresis to avoid overheating or excessive migration.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8jcc1","text":"Prepare an EtBr-free area for handling uncontaminated materials.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cri25","text":"Prepare ice for thawing samples.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6g70a","text":"EtBr is hazardous—handle with gloves and work in designated areas.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdkmd","text":"Avoid prolonged exposure to UV light, as it can damage DNA and cause skin burns.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmiq3","text":"Dispose of EtBr-contaminated materials in a dedicated waste bin.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ilhn","text":"Do not exceed recommended voltage or runtime to prevent overheating.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Preparing DNA Samples for Loading Remove DNA samples from the fridge and thaw in an ice bath. Note: Thawing too quickly may degrade DNA over time. 2. Prepare a strip of parafilm in the designated No EtBr area. 3. Aliquot 1 µL of 6X DNA loading dye onto the parafilm for each sample. The exact amount does not need to be precise. 4. Mix DNA with loading dye at a ratio of 4 µL DNA per 1 µL loading dye. Note: If using Taq polymerase with dye (e.g., Promega GoTaq Green), this step is not needed.

Preparing Agarose Gel Weigh out the agarose powder (amount varies based on the desired concentration). Agarose mass used: 0.6 g Higher concentrations are used for smaller DNA fragments. 2. Dissolve agarose in 1X TAE buffer (volume based on gel size). TAE buffer volume used: 75 mL Final agarose concentration: 0.8% 3. Microwave the mixture for 1–1.5 minutes, stopping to mix intermittently. Note: Watch closely to prevent boiling over.

Casting the Gel Seal the gel casting tray using tape or clamps to prevent leaks. Use two pieces of tape per side for a secure seal. 2. Add EtBr to the melted agarose and mix thoroughly. Amount added: 2.5 µL for 75 mL of gel (1 µL per 30 mL). 3. Place the comb into the gel casting tray to create sample wells. 4. Let the agarose cool slightly, then slowly pour it into the tray to avoid air bubbles. 5. Allow the gel to solidify (~20 minutes).

Running the Gel Carefully remove the tape and comb from the solidified gel. Place the gel in the gel rig, ensuring the wells face the negative (black) electrode. Add 1X TAE buffer to the gel rig until the gel is fully submerged. Load 5 µL of the DNA ladder into the first and last lanes of the gel. Ladder size: 1 kb Best Practices to Avoid Bubbles: When loading DNA samples or the ladder into the gel lanes, exercise caution to prevent air bubbles, which can displace the sample and cause it to spill into the buffer. Ensure the pipette tip is completely filled with the sample, eliminating any air gaps at the tip. If air pockets are present, gently press the pipette plunger to expel the excess air before loading. Slowly and steadily dispense the sample into the well to ensure smooth loading. 5. Load prepared DNA samples into wells between the ladder lanes. 6. Attach the power supply and set the following parameters: Voltage: 100-150V Current: 100 mAmps Run time: ~35 minutes 7. Verify bubble formation at the negative electrode, confirming proper current flow.

Gel Visualization Turn off the power supply and carefully remove the gel. Visualize the gel under a UV transilluminator. Note: Do not expose the gel to UV light longer than necessary, as excessive exposure may degrade DNA.

Expected Results Distinct bands corresponding to DNA fragments appear under UV light. Ladder bands allow estimation of fragment sizes. Properly run gels have clear, well-separated bands without smearing or excessive diffusion. Smearing or streaking may indicate degraded DNA or excessive voltage.
creator: Md Sahadat Ali
